Notes: Email from Guido Vittorio Di Gioacchino 30.05.2017. See 2017 data check file in UNHCR folder, row 46. A 22 year old woman from ivory coast, found unconscious on a dinghy was declared dead on the Aquarius. As reported she died after ingesting water and fuel. The deceased person has been identified by relatives (her cousin and his wife) on board; reportedly she has been rescued when she was unconscious and died after a while. At the dock, the cousin of the deceased woman called her husband, who lives in France, in order to inform him about the accident.

Article.title: Another forgotten shipwreck off Libya: 10 Libyans missing since 9 November 2020
Notes: Email sam fin <arous.libyan@gmail.com> on 4 june 2021, missingmigrants inbox - On 9.11.2020 11 Libyans embarked from Tripoli  around 20:00, the boat capsized around 22.30. The people did not drown but were waiting by the capsized boat until the next day (10.11) when they decided to swim back, the woman and girl stayed behing on top of the flipped boat. One of them (Salem Al-Sharif Farhat) saw a (black and blue) helicopter flying above his group. All men slowly disappeared on the way to shore, only one continued swimming for one day until he was rescued by fishing boat and brought back to Libya. He told the Libyan Coast Guard what had happened and they went to look for survivors the next day but didn't find anyone, they just found the boat .

Location.of.death: 43 miles off Lampedusa, Italy
Article.title: Migranti: due cadaveri e donna in gravi condizioni su barchino
Notes: Email from DI GIACOMO Flavio 03.03.2025 | POB from from Egypt, Bangladesh, Sudan, Somalia, Pakistan, Eritrea, and Ethiopia - incl. 2 women and 15 unaccompanied minors. They departed from Sabratha on the late evening of  24 February. Both women on board were taken to the Lampedusa medical facility, with one requiring hospitalization and the other suffering from fuel burns. The survivors were in a state of shock and recounted that they got lost at sea. Many were in the hold, where they suffocated due to the strong smell of fuel and lack of oxygen.

Article.title: At least one dead and three missing in the Mediterranean. Civilian monitoring vessel NADIR rescued 36 people from an unseaworthy boat and 12 more from the water hours later.
Notes: Eyewitnesses testify to witness one of the 4 missing drown. The ship left Sfax, Tunisia on 25 July and ran out of fuel two days after departure. Nine women (two pregnant), two infants, and two children were resued.
